title: OLTS Shipment
description: A normalized logistics shipment with full event history. OLTS v0.2 draft.

trackingNumber.description: Carrier-assigned tracking number (e.g. '73100059800035', '1Z999AA10123456784'). The primary key for this shipment.

carrierCode.description: Slug identifying the carrier (matches mappings/ directory).

service.description: Service level / product name as provided by carrier (e.g. 'express', 'parcel-de', 'UPS Ground', '顺丰特快').

currentStatus.description: Snapshot of the shipment's latest oltsCode. Convenience field; authoritative source is events[].oltsCode of the most recent event.

origin.description: Origin address (where the shipment was picked up).

destination.description: Destination address (where the shipment is being delivered).

estimatedDelivery.description: Estimated delivery as a date or a [from, through] window. Carriers like FedEx return a window; others return a single date.

actualDelivery.description: Actual delivery timestamp. ISO 8601 with timezone.

pieces.description: Sub-pieces of a multi-piece shipment. Omit for single-piece shipments. DHL/UPS support piece-level tracking; events[] may carry per-piece pieceId references.

events.description: Chronological tracking events. Order is carrier-dependent; consumers SHOULD sort by eventTime before display.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. CNY, USD, EUR).

declaredValue.description: Declared value for customs / insurance. Mostly used for cross-border shipments.

isReturn.description: true if this shipment is a return / reverse logistics flow.

metadata.description: Carrier-specific extension fields not covered by the standard properties.

name.description: Recipient/sender name (may be masked by carrier policy).

addressLines.description: Free-form address lines (street, suite, etc.) in local language order. Many domestic Chinese carriers return only city/district granularity.

Address.description: Postal address. All fields optional — provide what the carrier returns. SHOULD respect carrier-side masking (don't unmask phone numbers/names that were masked upstream).
